title: Classic sausage rolls
subtitle: Pork sausage meat in flaky puff pastry
excerpt: Sausage rolls made with seasoned pork sausage meat wrapped in ready-rolled puff pastry and baked until the pastry is shatteringly crisp and deep golden. Served warm from the oven.
sourceNotes: Sausage rolls as a British bakehouse and picnic staple appear in domestic manuals from the nineteenth century. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136, documents meat-filled pastry rolls as a standard preparation for cold collation. The form using a puff pastry wrap around seasoned pork sausage meat became standard in the twentieth century and is now a British food staple, sold in bakeries and at events throughout the country.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze unbaked: assemble, freeze on a tray, then bag. Bake from frozen at 200°C for 30 minutes.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best served warm from the oven. Reheat baked rolls at 180°C for 8 minutes.

content.text: The pastry must be cold when it goes into the oven. If the butter in the layers has softened, the pastry bakes greasy and doesn't puff. Work quickly: take the pastry out of the fridge only when the filling is ready, and get the assembled rolls onto the tray and into the oven within 10 minutes. Score the top of each roll two or three times with a sharp knife: this lets steam escape and gives the filling room to expand without splitting the pastry seam.

Heat the oven to 200°C fan. Line a large baking tray with baking paper. Mix the sausage meat, grated onion, thyme, salt, and pepper together in a bowl until combined. Squeeze out any excess moisture from the grated onion before adding.

Unroll the cold puff pastry on a lightly floured surface and cut lengthways into two long strips. Divide the sausage meat filling into two portions and shape each into a long cylinder along the centre of each pastry strip. Brush one long edge of each strip with beaten egg. Fold the other edge over the filling to meet the egg-washed edge and press to seal firmly. Turn seam-side down. Cut each roll into 6 pieces. Score two slashes in the top of each piece. Brush all over with beaten egg. Place on the lined tray and bake for 22 to 25 minutes, until the pastry is deep golden and the filling is cooked through. Cool on a rack for 5 minutes before serving.
